name: Censure Road Camp
intro: The Censure road camp sits between two dead routes: one back to Ash Chapel, one toward the silent Hallowfen bells.

ambient: Confession after bell check. God can wait ten breaths. The road cannot.
If that slate falls in the mud, nobody is eating until I can read it.
No seal, no ration. A sad face is not a seal.
Road prices are fair prices, if you ask the road.
Again. A cultist will not wait while you remember where your elbow lives.
One wrong peal sends a squad to the latrines. Ask me how I learned.
If the board says missing, it means late. If it says late, it means dead.
I am not afraid. I am conserving bravery for paid work.
If you bleed on the tent flap, wipe it before you faint.
A crate gets a destination. A porter gets a shrug. I envy crates.
Do not touch the bags. One of them already hummed at me.
Tether line is empty. The goats filed a complaint and escaped.
I wash camp linen. I do not wash camp stories.

interact.log: Father Odran's private tent smells of lamp oil, damp canvas, and shaving soap.

lock.title: Father Odran's Tent
lines: A private flap is tied with ledger cord. The knot is ordinary. The consequences are not.

methods.label: Open the ledger cord
methods.successLog: The cord comes loose without snapping the wax fleck.
methods.failLog: The knot holds, and the canvas gives a loud dry pop.

search.title: Father Odran's Tent
lines: A cot, a travel chest, and folded church linen fill the private tent.

methods.label: Search the folded linen
methods.successLog: Under a stack of priestly cloth, you find a woman's underlinen tucked hard against the chest wall.
methods.failLog: The linen is folded too tightly. If there is a secret here, it stays flat.

success.log: The hidden underlinen gives you a reason to watch Father Odran's tent after last bell.

interact.log: Fresh writ copies are stacked by route: chapel, road camp, Hallowfen wall. The last stack is still tied.
The camp bell hangs from a patched road mast. Each peal marks one route, one alarm, or one body count.
The east road is chained until Preceptor Voss signs the marching slate.
The screen is canvas over a spare fence frame. Someone scratched a price table into the wood and then crossed it out.
The board sorts orders by road bell. Hallowfen has three missed marks and one ink smear where a name was cut out.
Runa has split the ledger into issue, debt, and excuses. Excuses has the longest column.
The tether line is empty except for chewed rope and one offended hoofprint.
The evidence shed is sealed with three tags: cult relics, road bodies, and objects that talked back.
Three water barrels sit under wax seals. One seal has tooth marks and a guilty thumbprint.
A prayer slip nailed to the latrine post asks God for mercy and better hinges.
The south road drops back toward the long ash fields and the chapel breach.
